sections.title: Intake
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Mithril Protocol continuous development and Intersect budget administration.

Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, with project-specific management through Intersect and a PSSC payment address.
Deliverables: Mithril Protocol Continuous Development, Intersect Budget Administration

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, split into 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling nodes and applications to verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that full node synchronisation is slow and light clients rely on centralized trust, making secure access and verification of blockchain state a pressing infrastructure challenge.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer claims Mithril will lower onboarding friction for exchanges, wallets, and monitoring by enabling faster sync times.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and references voting documentation, audited results, and an audit report.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The requested amount is asserted not to breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713, either alone or in aggregate at submission time.
missing: Independent evidence for: Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling nodes and applications to verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that full node synchronisation is slow and light clients rely on centralized trust, making secure access and verification of blockchain state a pressing infrastructure challenge.
Independent evidence for: The proposer claims Mithril will lower onboarding friction for exchanges, wallets, and monitoring by enabling faster sync times.

Independent evidence for: Audit and oversight costs are included in the proposal overhead, with independent oversight to be provided through Intersect and technically capable third parties, including reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, split into 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee." though 1 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling nodes and applications to verify state rapidly without relying on the entire chain." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
